PTBP1 functions as a suppressor of ferroptosis in endometrial carcinoma cells by stabilizing SLC7A11 mRNA

evidence_entities.text: PTBP1 knockdown significantly suppressed tumor growth in vivo
PTBP1 knockdown significantly increased ROS levels, reduced intracellular GSH concentrations, and elevated levels of both MDA and ferrous ions
PTBP1 binds directly to SLC7A11 mRNA and stabilizes it
The half-life of SLC7A11 mRNA was significantly shortened upon PTBP1 disruption

notes: This study establishes PTBP1 as a key regulator of ferroptosis in endometrial carcinoma through stabilization of SLC7A11 mRNA
The PTBP1-SLC7A11 axis represents a novel therapeutic target for endometrial cancer treatment
